text:
The reference "Optimal voting" by Ben Armstrong (2025) at `https://pypi.org/project/optimal-voting` is cited in the reference list of a real academic paper ("What Voting Rules Actually Do: A Data-Driven Analysis of Multi-Winner Voting" on arXiv), and Ben Armstrong is a verified researcher in voting theory. The PyPI package URL matches the cited URL exactly. This is a software/package citation (a non-academic source), and the author attribution matches a real researcher in this domain. Although the PyPI page itself returned a JavaScript challenge (preventing direct rendering), the reference is corroborated by its citation in a real paper with the same URL and author.

EXPLANATION: This is a non-academic software/PyPI package citation. The reference "Optimal voting" by Ben Armstrong (2025) at https://pypi.org/project/optimal-voting is cited verbatim (with the exact same URL and author) in a real academic paper on arXiv (2508.06454), and Ben Armstrong is a verifiable researcher in voting and social choice. The citation is a legitimate software package reference, not a fabricated academic paper.
LINK: https://pypi.org/project/optimal-voting
FOUND_TITLE: optimal-voting
FOUND_AUTHORS: Ben Armstrong
FOUND_YEAR: 2025
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
